title: MD
biography: Wu Lien-teh was a Malaysian-Chinese physician who stopped the Manchurian plague epidemic of 1910-1911, the first person to identify pneumonic plague transmission by respiratory droplets. He invented the modern surgical face mask and was the first person of Chinese descent nominated for the Nobel Prize.
aiSummary: Wu Lien-teh was an epidemiology specialist who stopped the 1910 Manchurian plague and invented the surgical face mask. First Chinese Nobel Prize nominee. Based in Harbin, China.

timeline.title: Appointed to stop Manchurian plague
timeline.description: At age 31, tasked with halting a deadly pneumonic plague epidemic in Manchuria

timeline.title: Stopped the Manchurian plague
timeline.description: Identified respiratory transmission, invented the face mask, and implemented quarantine measures that ended the epidemic

timeline.title: Chaired International Plague Conference
timeline.description: Organized the first international scientific conference held in China

timeline.title: Founded Harbin Medical University
timeline.description: Established one of China's leading medical schools
